description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that a valid order is created correctly
  So that the trade lifecycle begins with a clean, auditable order state

elements.name: QA submits a new equity buy order

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
market data is live for symbol "AAPL"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
no existing open orders for symbol "AAPL"
the QA submits a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
an order ID is assigned immediately
the order appears in the blotter with status "New"
the side is "Buy", quantity is 100, and limit price is 150.00
the audit trail records the submission with timestamp and user ID

elements.name: QA submits a new equity sell order

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
market data is live for symbol "AAPL"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
no existing open orders for symbol "AAPL"
the QA submits a sell order for 50 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 155.00
an order ID is assigned immediately
the order appears in the blotter with status "New"
the side is "Sell", quantity is 50, and limit price is 155.00
the audit trail records the submission with timestamp and user ID

elements.name: Order submission is rejected when quantity is zero

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
market data is live for symbol "AAPL"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the QA submits a buy order for 0 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
the order is not created
an error message is displayed explaining the invalid quantity
no order ID is assigned

elements.name: Order submission is rejected when price is negative

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
market data is live for symbol "AAPL"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the QA submits a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price -1.00
the order is not created
an error message is displayed explaining the invalid price
no order ID is assigned

elements.name: Order submission is rejected for an unknown symbol

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
market data is live for symbol "AAPL"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the QA submits a buy order for 100 shares of "FAKESYM" at limit price 100.00
the order is not created
an error message is displayed explaining the unknown instrument
no order ID is assigned

name: Add New Order

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that an open order can be modified correctly
  So that price and quantity changes are reflected with full chain integrity

elements.name: QA modifies the price of an open order

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
the order status is "New"
the QA modifies the limit price to 152.00
the order is updated with the new limit price 152.00
the order quantity remains 100
the order status remains "New"
the original order and the replacement are linked in order history
the audit trail records the modify action with timestamp and user ID

elements.name: QA modifies the quantity of an open order

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
the order status is "New"
the QA modifies the quantity to 200
the order is updated with the new quantity 200
the limit price remains 150.00
the order status remains "New"
the original order and the replacement are linked in order history

elements.name: Modify is rejected when order is already filled

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has status "Filled"
the QA attempts to modify the limit price to 155.00
the modify request is rejected
the order status remains "Filled"
a clear rejection reason is shown to the QA user

elements.name: Modify is rejected when order is already cancelled

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has status "Canceled"
the QA attempts to modify the limit price to 155.00
the modify request is rejected
the order status remains "Canceled"
a clear rejection reason is shown to the QA user

name: Modify Order

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that an open order can be cancelled cleanly
  So that no unjustified executions occur after cancellation

elements.name: QA cancels an open order

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 200 shares of "MSFT" at limit price 300.00
the order status is "New"
the QA submits a cancel request for the order
the system acknowledges the cancel request
the order status transitions to "Canceled"
no fill execution is recorded after the cancel timestamp
the audit trail records the cancel event with timestamp and user ID

elements.name: Cancel is rejected for an already-filled order

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 50 shares of "MSFT" has status "Filled"
the QA attempts to cancel the filled order
the cancel request is rejected
the order status remains "Filled"
a clear rejection reason is shown to the QA user

elements.name: Cancel is rejected for an already-cancelled order

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 50 shares of "MSFT" has status "Canceled"
the QA attempts to cancel the already-cancelled order
the cancel request is rejected
the order status remains "Canceled"
a clear rejection reason is shown to the QA user

elements.name: Cancel of a partially filled order leaves correct state

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 500 shares of "MSFT" at limit price 300.00
the order has been partially filled for 200 shares
the order status is "Partially Filled"
the QA submits a cancel request for the remaining quantity
the system acknowledges the cancel request
the order status transitions to "Partially Filled and Canceled"
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) remains 200
the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) becomes 0
the audit trail records the cancel event with timestamp and user ID

name: Cancel Order

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that rejected orders show meaningful rejection reasons
  So that QA can distinguish business rule rejections from system errors

elements.name: Order is rejected for invalid field values

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the QA submits a buy order with quantity set to 0
the order status becomes "Rejected"
the rejection reason displayed contains "invalid quantity"
no position impact is recorded for the order
no settlement record is created for the order

elements.name: Order is rejected for invalid field values

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the QA submits a buy order with price set to -1.00
the order status becomes "Rejected"
the rejection reason displayed contains "invalid price"
no position impact is recorded for the order
no settlement record is created for the order

elements.name: Order is rejected for invalid field values

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the QA submits a buy order with symbol set to FAKESYM
the order status becomes "Rejected"
the rejection reason displayed contains "unknown instrument"
no position impact is recorded for the order
no settlement record is created for the order

elements.name: Order is rejected for invalid field values

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the QA submits a buy order with account set to BADACCT
the order status becomes "Rejected"
the rejection reason displayed contains "invalid account"
no position impact is recorded for the order
no settlement record is created for the order

elements.name: Exchange rejects order for price out of limit up/down band

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" is submitted at price 9999.00
the exchange simulator rejects the order with reason "Price out of band"
the order status becomes "Rejected"
the rejection reason displayed contains "Price out of band"
no position impact is recorded for the order

elements.name: Rejected order does not appear in settlement report

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been rejected
the settlement report is generated or refreshed
no settlement record exists for the rejected order

name: Order Reject

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that a partial execution updates order fields correctly
  So that CumQty, LeavesQty, AvgPx, and status are all accurate

elements.name: Order receives a single partial fill

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 500 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
the order status is "New"
a fill execution arrives for 200 shares at price 149.80
the order status becomes "Partially Filled"
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 200
the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) is 300
the average price (AvgPx) is 149.80
the execution appears in order history with correct quantity and price
the position is updated to reflect 200 shares acquired

elements.name: Order receives two sequential partial fills

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 500 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
the order status is "New"
a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 149.50
a fill execution arrives for 150 shares at price 150.00
the order status is "Partially Filled"
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 250
the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) is 250
the average price (AvgPx) is 149.80
both executions appear in order history
the position reflects 250 shares acquired

elements.name: Partial fill does not allow modification of filled quantity

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order for 500 shares of "AAPL" has been partially filled for 200 shares
the QA attempts to modify the quantity to 150
the modify request is rejected
a clear rejection reason explains the quantity is below the filled amount

name: Partial Fill

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that a fully filled order reaches the correct terminal state
  So that CumQty, LeavesQty, status, and position are all accurate and the order is closed

elements.name: Order is fully filled in a single execution

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "TSLA" at limit price 200.00
the order status is "New"
a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 199.50
the order status becomes "Filled"
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 100
the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) is 0
the average price (AvgPx) is 199.50
the order is closed and no further modifications are possible
the position reflects 100 shares of "TSLA" acquired at average price 199.50

elements.name: Order is fully filled across two partial executions

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 300 shares of "TSLA" at limit price 200.00
the order status is "New"
a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 199.00
a fill execution arrives for 200 shares at price 200.00
the order status becomes "Filled"
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 300
the leaves quantity (LeavesQty) is 0
the average price (AvgPx) is 199.67
both executions appear in order history
the position reflects 300 shares of "TSLA" acquired

elements.name: Filled order cannot be cancelled

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "TSLA" has status "Filled"
the QA attempts to cancel the filled order
the cancel request is rejected
the order status remains "Filled"

elements.name: Filled order cannot be modified

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "TSLA" has status "Filled"
the QA attempts to modify the limit price to 210.00
the modify request is rejected
the order status remains "Filled"

name: Full Fill

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that replayed execution reports are safely ignored
  So that positions and order state are not double-counted after FIX session recovery

elements.name: Replayed execution report is safely ignored

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled
the fill execution has ExecID "EXEC-001"
the exchange simulator replays the same execution with ExecID "EXEC-001"
the duplicate execution is detected and rejected
the order status remains "Filled"
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) remains 100
only one execution record exists in order history for ExecID "EXEC-001"
the position is not double-counted

elements.name: Duplicate partial fill is safely ignored

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 500 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
a fill execution with ExecID "EXEC-002" has already been processed for 200 shares
the exchange simulator replays the same execution with ExecID "EXEC-002"
the duplicate is detected and ignored
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) remains 200
the order status remains "Partially Filled"
only one execution record exists for ExecID "EXEC-002"

elements.name: Duplicate detection is logged for audit purposes

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled
the fill execution has ExecID "EXEC-003"
the exchange simulator replays the same execution with ExecID "EXEC-003"
the duplicate rejection event is recorded in the application log
the log entry contains the ExecID "EXEC-003" and the order ID

name: Duplicate Execution Protection

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that fill quantities exceeding the order quantity are blocked
  So that positions and books are never overstated

elements.name: Overfill attempt is blocked

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
the order status is "New"
a fill execution arrives for 150 shares at price 150.00
the overfill is detected by the system
the order quantity ceiling is enforced
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) does not exceed 100
an overfill alert or error is visible to the QA user
the overfill event is recorded in the application log

elements.name: Overfill after partial fill is blocked

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 200 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
a fill execution has already been processed for 150 shares
the order status is "Partially Filled"
a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 150.00
the overfill is detected by the system
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) does not exceed 200
the overfill event is recorded in the application log

elements.name: Exact fill quantity equal to order quantity is accepted

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
an open buy order exists for 100 shares of "AAPL" at limit price 150.00
a fill execution arrives for 100 shares at price 150.00
the order status becomes "Filled"
the cumulative quantity (CumQty) is 100
no overfill alert is raised

name: Overfill Protection

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that filled trades appear correctly in the settlement report
  So that settlement instructions are accurate and the T+2 cycle is respected

elements.name: Filled trade appears correctly in the settlement report

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
today's trade date is a valid settlement business day
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00
the settlement report is generated or refreshed
the trade appears in the settlement report
the report row contains:
the trade date matches today's date
the settlement date is trade date plus 2 business days

elements.name: Cancelled order does not appear in settlement report

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
today's trade date is a valid settlement business day
a buy order for 200 shares of "AAPL" has been cancelled before any fill
the settlement report is generated or refreshed
no settlement record exists for the cancelled order

elements.name: Rejected order does not appear in settlement report

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
today's trade date is a valid settlement business day
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been rejected
the settlement report is generated or refreshed
no settlement record exists for the rejected order

elements.name: Partially filled and cancelled order appears with correct executed quantity

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
today's trade date is a valid settlement business day
a buy order for 500 shares of "AAPL" was partially filled for 200 shares then cancelled
the settlement report is generated or refreshed
the settlement record shows quantity 200
the settlement record does not include the unfilled 300 shares

name: Settlement Report

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that RHUB records match source trade details exactly
  So that reconciliation breaks are caught before they reach downstream systems

elements.name: RHUB record matches the source trade

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00
the trade appears correctly in the settlement report
the RHUB processing run has completed
a RHUB record exists for the trade
the RHUB symbol matches "AAPL"
the RHUB quantity matches 100
the RHUB price matches 150.00
the RHUB account matches "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT"
the RHUB settlement date matches the settlement report settlement date
no reconciliation break is flagged for this trade

elements.name: No RHUB record exists for a cancelled order

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
a buy order for 200 shares of "AAPL" has been cancelled before any fill
the RHUB processing run has completed
no RHUB record exists for the cancelled order

elements.name: RHUB reconciliation break is raised for a mismatched quantity

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00
the RHUB record for the trade has an incorrect quantity of 50
the QA checks the RHUB reconciliation status
a reconciliation break is flagged for the trade
the break details show the expected quantity 100 and actual quantity 50

name: RHUB Validation

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that order history captures every lifecycle event in sequence
  So that any defect can be diagnosed from the audit trail

elements.name: Order history captures a simple new and fill sequence

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00
the QA opens the order history for the order
the history contains exactly 2 events in sequence
event 1 has type "New" with quantity 100 and price 150.00
event 2 has type "Filled" with cumulative quantity 100
each event has a timestamp that is later than the previous event
each event records the user ID of the actor

elements.name: Order history captures the full add, modify, fill sequence

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the following actions have been performed on a single order:
the QA opens the order history for the order
the history contains exactly 3 events in correct sequence
the events appear in the order: New, Replace, Filled
each event has a timestamp that is later than the previous event
each event records the user ID of the actor

elements.name: Order history captures partial fill then cancel sequence

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the following actions have been performed on a single order:
the QA opens the order history for the order
the history contains exactly 3 events in correct sequence
the events appear in the order: New, Partially Filled, Canceled
the final status shown is "Partially Filled and Canceled"

elements.name: Order history is read-only and cannot be edited

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00
the QA opens the order history for the order
no edit or delete controls are available on any history event

name: Order History

description:
As a QA analyst
  I need to verify that positions reflect only executed trade quantities
  So that the position book is accurate and free from phantom or duplicate entries

elements.name: Position reflects only executed quantities

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the starting position for "AAPL" in account "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT" is 0 shares
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" is fully filled at 150.00
a buy order for 50 shares of "AAPL" is partially filled for 30 shares
a buy order for 200 shares of "AAPL" is submitted and then cancelled before any fill
the net position for "AAPL" is 130 shares
the position does not include quantity from the cancelled order
the position does not include the unfilled 20 shares from the partial order

elements.name: Rejected order has no position impact

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the starting position for "MSFT" in account "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT" is 0 shares
a buy order for 100 shares of "MSFT" is submitted with an invalid price
the order is rejected by the system
the net position for "MSFT" remains 0 shares

elements.name: Sell order reduces position correctly

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the starting position for "AAPL" in account "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT" is 200 shares
a sell order for 50 shares of "AAPL" is fully filled at 155.00
the net position for "AAPL" is 150 shares

elements.name: Position updates are visible immediately after fill

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
the starting position for "TSLA" in account "QA_TEST_ACCOUNT" is 0 shares
a buy order for 100 shares of "TSLA" is fully filled at 200.00
the net position for "TSLA" is 100 shares within 5 seconds of the fill

elements.name: Position management shows no duplicate entries

steps.name: the QA user is logged in to the alpha environment
the environment banner confirms "ALPHA — NON-PRODUCTION"
the QA account has entitlement to place equity orders
a buy order for 100 shares of "AAPL" has been fully filled at 150.00
the QA views the position detail for "AAPL"
each execution appears exactly once in the position detail
the total position quantity equals the sum of all unique fill quantities

name: Position Management

description:
As a QA engineer
  I want to validate resilience and boundary conditions
  So that the system behaves correctly under non-ideal circumstances

elements.name: Order submitted while FIX session is disconnected

steps.name: the QA user is logged in
the FIX session is forcibly disconnected

result.error_message:

the order should be blocked or rejected with a connectivity error

elements.name: Session drops after order send but before acknowledgment

steps.name: the QA user is logged in
a buy order for 200 shares of MSFT at 400.00 has been submitted
the FIX session drops immediately after order submission

result.error_message:
Error: [KNOWN ISSUE] Mock processes orders synchronously in-memory. Mid-flight disconnect cannot be simulated.
Fix needed: Async order pipeline with durable message queue (e.g. RabbitMQ) and reconnect/replay logic.

the order should remain in its current state without duplication

elements.name: Cancel request crosses with fill in flight

steps.name: an open buy order exists for 500 shares of TSLA at 200.00
a cancel request is sent simultaneously with a fill for 500 shares
the final state should be either "Filled" or "Canceled" — never both
the audit trail should reflect the actual event sequence

elements.name: Modify request arrives after order is fully filled

steps.name: an order for 100 shares of AAPL at 150.00 has been fully filled
a modify request is sent to change quantity to 200 shares
the modify should be rejected with reason "Order already filled"
the order status should remain "Filled"
